## **4.2  Data Flow: Map Screen (Primary User Journey)**

The complete lifecycle of bathroom data from database to rendered map pin:

1. User opens app â†’ \_layout.tsx mounts â†’ SplashScreen.preventAutoHideAsync() called.

2. AuthContext initialises â†’ BOOTSTRAPPING â†’ supabase.auth.getSession() â†’ GUEST or SESSION\_RECOVERY.

3. SplashScreen.hideAsync() called once status \!== BOOTSTRAPPING.

4. Map tab renders â†’ useLocation() hook requests ACCESS\_FINE\_LOCATION permission.

5. On permission grant â†’ watchPositionAsync() starts â†’ LocationContext updated.

6. useBathrooms({ lat, lng, radius: 1000 }) hook fires â†’ TanStack Query checks staleTime (5min).

7. Cache MISS â†’ Supabase RPC call: select \* from get\_bathrooms\_near(lat, lng, 1000).

8. Supabase evaluates RLS: bathrooms\_select\_public policy â†’ moderation\_status \= "active" enforced at DB level.

9. Response arrives â†’ zod schema validates shape â†’ TanStack Query stores in memory cache.

10. CacheManager.set() persists to AsyncStorage with TTL (5 min hard, 1 min stale).

11. MapView component re-renders with bathroom markers.

12. User taps marker â†’ Reanimated BottomSheet animates up â†’ bathroom/\[id\] detail data fetched.

13. Detail view calls v\_bathroom\_detail\_public view â†’ includes code summary if code exists.

14. Code value rendered via CodeBadge component (visibility: shown / hidden / premium\_gated).

## **4.3  Data Flow: Protected Action (Favorite Toggle)**

15. Guest user taps heart icon on BathroomCard.

16. useFavorites().toggle() calls useProtectedAction() hook.

17. useProtectedAction detects isGuest â†’ calls returnIntent.set({ type: "favorite\_toggle", route: "/bathroom/\[id\]", params: { bathroom\_id } }).

18. Router.push("/(auth)/login") â€” modal slides up.

19. User signs in â†’ Supabase SIGNED\_IN event fires â†’ AuthContext transitions to AUTHENTICATED\_USER.

20. useProtectedRoute in auth layout detects auth â†’ checks returnIntent.consume().

21. Intent found â†’ replay\_strategy: "immediate\_after\_auth" â†’ execute favorite toggle automatically.

22. useMutation calls supabase.from("favorites").insert({ user\_id, bathroom\_id }).

23. RLS policy favorites\_insert\_own: auth.uid() \= user\_id â€” enforced at DB level.

24. On success â†’ queryClient.invalidateQueries(\["favorites"\]) â†’ UI updates optimistically.

25. On network failure â†’ offlineQueue.enqueue("favorite\_add", { bathroom\_id }, userId).

26. When connectivity restores (NetInfo or AppState) â†’ offlineQueue.process() retries.

## **4.4  RLS Policy Reference â€” Enforced at DB Level**

These policies are the single source of truth for data access. No client-side filtering should be relied upon as a security boundary.

| Table | Operation | Policy Condition | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| bathrooms | SELECT | moderation\_status \= "active" | Public read. No auth required. Unauthenticated guests see all active bathrooms. |
| bathrooms | INSERT | auth.uid() \= created\_by | Authenticated only. Validate created\_by \= current user server-side. |
| bathroom\_access\_codes | SELECT | visibility \= "visible" AND lifecycle \= "active" | Public read. No auth required. |
| bathroom\_access\_codes | INSERT | auth.uid() \= submitted\_by | Authenticated only. |
| code\_votes | ALL | auth.uid() \= user\_id | Full CRUD for own votes only. DB UNIQUE(code\_id, user\_id) prevents double-voting. |
| favorites | SELECT | auth.uid() \= user\_id | User sees only own favorites. |
| favorites | INSERT | auth.uid() \= user\_id | Authenticated only. |
| favorites | DELETE | auth.uid() \= user\_id | Own favorites only. |
| profiles | SELECT | auth.uid() \= id | Users read only own profile. Admin reads via service role key (server-side only). |
| profiles | UPDATE | auth.uid() \= id | Users update own profile only. role field must be immutable from client â€” add server-side constraint. |
| bathroom\_reports | INSERT | auth.uid() \= reported\_by | Authenticated only. One report per user per bathroom recommended â€” add UNIQUE constraint. |
| business\_claims | ALL | auth.uid() \= claimant\_user\_id | Authenticated. review\_status must be immutable from client â€” server only via service role. |

**ðŸš¨  CRITICAL:** profiles.role must never be updatable from the client. Add a Supabase check: create policy "profiles\_update\_role\_blocked" on profiles for update using (auth.uid() \= id) with check (role \= OLD.role).

## **4.5  Offline State Flow**

| Scenario | Behaviour |
| :---- | :---- |
| **App launches offline** | CacheManager.get() serves stale data (is\_stale \= true). OfflineBanner shows. Map renders from AsyncStorage. |
| **Mutation attempted offline** | useMutation onError callback detects network error â†’ offlineQueue.enqueue(). Toast: "Saved. Will sync when online." |
| **App comes online (foreground)** | AppState "active" event â†’ offlineQueue.process() â†’ execute pending mutations. |
| **Network restores in background** | NetInfo isConnected â†’ true â†’ offlineQueue.process() triggered. |
| **Mutation replay succeeds** | Remove from queue. Invalidate relevant TanStack Query keys. Toast: "Synced." |
| **Mutation replay fails (3x)** | Remove from queue with MAX\_RETRIES exceeded. Toast: "Action could not be synced. Please retry." |
| **Session expires while offline** | On next online event, Supabase auth refresh â†’ if fails â†’ SESSION\_INVALID â†’ queue cleared â†’ sign out. |

# **Â§5  Critical Fixes Required Before Phase 3**

These are confirmed bugs and misalignments found in the existing codebase that will cause production failures if not resolved.

## **5.1  Environment Variable Mismatch â€” SEVERITY: P0**

**ðŸš¨  CRITICAL:** .env.local exports EXPO\_PUBLIC\_SUPABASE\_KEY but src/lib/supabase.ts reads EXPO\_PUBLIC\_SUPABASE\_ANON\_KEY. The Supabase client will silently use an empty string, causing all API calls to fail in the production build.

Fix: Standardise all references to EXPO\_PUBLIC\_SUPABASE\_ANON\_KEY across:

* .env.local â€” rename EXPO\_PUBLIC\_SUPABASE\_KEY â†’ EXPO\_PUBLIC\_SUPABASE\_ANON\_KEY

* src/lib/supabase.ts â€” already reads EXPO\_PUBLIC\_SUPABASE\_ANON\_KEY (correct)

* app.config.ts extra block â€” update key name

* utils/supabase.ts â€” legacy file, reads EXPO\_PUBLIC\_SUPABASE\_KEY. Delete or align.

* src/constants/config.ts â€” no supabase key reference (correct, do not add one)

## **5.2  Duplicate Supabase Client â€” SEVERITY: P0**

**ðŸš¨  CRITICAL:** Two supabase.ts files exist: utils/supabase.ts (legacy, root-level) and src/lib/supabase.ts (canonical). Both export a supabase client. Having two Supabase clients creates two separate auth state listeners and two separate session stores â€” this will cause random sign-out loops.

Fix:

27. Delete utils/supabase.ts entirely.

28. Ensure all imports use: import { supabase } from "@/lib/supabase"

29. Check App.tsx (root) â€” it imports from "../utils/supabase". App.tsx appears to be a legacy file. Delete it. Expo Router uses app/\_layout.tsx as the entry point.

## **5.3  processLock Removed in Supabase JS 2.39+ â€” SEVERITY: P1**

**âš **  utils/supabase.ts passes lock: processLock to createClient. processLock was deprecated and removed in @supabase/supabase-js 2.39. This will throw a TypeScript error and break the build.

Fix: Remove lock: processLock from the createClient options. The canonical src/lib/supabase.ts already omits it correctly.

## **5.4  TabBarIcon Uses require() Inside Component â€” SEVERITY: P1**

**âš **  app/(tabs)/\_layout.tsx uses a function component that calls require("react-native") on every render. This is an anti-pattern that breaks Fast Refresh and can cause build failures with Hermes.

Fix: Import View and Text at the top of the file. Replace TabBarIcon placeholder with @expo/vector-icons Ionicons component.

## **5.5  Missing SplashScreen Control â€” SEVERITY: P1**

**âš **  app/\_layout.tsx has no SplashScreen.preventAutoHideAsync() call. The splash screen will flash and disappear before authentication bootstrap completes, causing a white screen flicker on every cold start.

Fix: Add to app/\_layout.tsx:

import \* as SplashScreen from 'expo-splash-screen';

SplashScreen.preventAutoHideAsync(); // Call before component mount

Then in RootLayout, watch sessionState.status and call SplashScreen.hideAsync() when status \!== "BOOTSTRAPPING".

## **5.6  app.json vs app.config.ts Conflict â€” SEVERITY: P2**

**âš **  Both app.json and app.config.ts exist. Expo uses app.config.ts when both are present, but app.json has divergent settings: supportsTablet: true (should be false per spec), NSLocationAlwaysAndWhenInUseUsageDescription (not in app.config.ts), and iosGoogleMapsApiKey (empty string, not needed).

Fix: Delete app.json. app.config.ts is the canonical config. Ensure app.config.ts has all required fields. The always-on location permission string in app.json was a Phase 1 overstep â€” remove it.

## **5.7  Missing metro.config.js â€” SEVERITY: P0 for NativeWind**

**ðŸš¨  CRITICAL:** NativeWind v4 requires a metro.config.js with withNativeWind() wrapper. Without it, all className props on React Native components are silently ignored â€” every NativeWind style is a no-op.

Required metro.config.js:

const { getDefaultConfig } \= require('expo/metro-config');

const { withNativeWind } \= require('nativewind/metro');

const config \= getDefaultConfig(\_\_dirname);

module.exports \= withNativeWind(config, { input: './global.css' });

Required global.css:

@tailwind base;

@tailwind components;

@tailwind utilities;

payload.message:
**Phase 4 Spec**

This spec targets the current seams in [AuthContext.tsx](C:/Users/T/.codex/worktrees/e8ab/PeeDom/src/contexts/AuthContext.tsx), [offline-queue.ts](C:/Users/T/.codex/worktrees/e8ab/PeeDom/src/lib/offline-queue.ts), [query-client.ts](C:/Users/T/.codex/worktrees/e8ab/PeeDom/src/lib/query-client.ts), and the active Expo config in [app.config.ts](C:/Users/T/.codex/worktrees/e8ab/PeeDom/app.config.ts). [app.json](C:/Users/T/.codex/worktrees/e8ab/PeeDom/app.json) still exists, so Claude must either delete it or keep it identical to the dynamic config to avoid config drift.

**1. Dependency Resolution Map**

Run these exact commands:

```bash
npx expo install @sentry/react-native
npx expo install @react-native-community/netinfo
npx expo install @gorhom/bottom-sheet
npx expo install zod
npx expo install zustand
```

Then run:

```bash
npx expo install --check
```

Requirements:
- `@gorhom/bottom-sheet` must use the existing Expo-compatible `react-native-reanimated` and `react-native-gesture-handler`. Do not manually pin peer versions outside Expo resolution.
- `zod` and `zustand` are JS-only, but still install them through `expo install` so the lockfile is resolved through the project’s Expo tooling.
- Sentry config must be applied to [app.config.ts](C:/Users/T/.codex/worktrees/e8ab/PeeDom/app.config.ts). If [app.json](C:/Users/T/.codex/worktrees/e8ab/PeeDom/app.json) remains, mirror the same change there.

Required Sentry app config shape:

```json
{
  "expo": {
    "plugins": [
      [
        "@sentry/react-native/expo",
        {
          "organization": "<SENTRY_ORG>",
          "project": "<SENTRY_PROJECT>",
          "url": "https://sentry.io/"
        }
      ]
    ],
    "extra": {
      "EXPO_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN": "<public-dsn>"
    }
  }
}
```

Sentry rules:
- `organization` and `project` are build metadata, not secrets.
- `SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN` must live in EAS/build env only. Never place it in app config.
- Keep `EXPO_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN` env-backed and public; runtime init remains in [sentry.ts](C:/Users/T/.codex/worktrees/e8ab/PeeDom/src/lib/sentry.ts).

**2. Auth Decomposition Blueprint**

Create this structure:
- `C:\Users\T\.codex\worktrees\e8ab\PeeDom\src\features\auth\useAuthStore.ts`
- `C:\Users\T\.codex\worktrees\e8ab\PeeDom\src\features\auth\AuthService.ts`
- `C:\Users\T\.codex\worktrees\e8ab\PeeDom\src\features\auth\AuthProvider.tsx`
- `C:\Users\T\.codex\worktrees\e8ab\PeeDom\src\features\auth\index.ts`

Transition rule:
- Reduce [AuthContext.tsx](C:/Users/T/.codex/worktrees/e8ab/PeeDom/src/contexts/AuthContext.tsx) to a compatibility export only during migration, then delete it once imports are moved.

Responsibilities:
- `useAuthStore`: readable auth state only. Hold `status`, `session`, `user`, `profile`, `loading`, `lastAuthError`, and derived selectors like `isGuest`, `isAuthenticated`, `canAccessProtectedRoute`.
- `AuthProvider`: owns bootstrap, `supabase.auth.onAuthStateChange`, queue/user-scope handoff, and cleanup. It does not fetch directly from components and does not navigate.
- `AuthService`: owns every Supabase call. No React imports. No Zustand imports.

`AuthService` must expose:
- `getSession()`
- `getUser()`
- `getProfile(userId)`
- `signInWithEmail(payload)`
- `signUpWithEmail(payload)`
- `signOutRemote()`
- `signOutLocal()`
- `resolveAuthSnapshot(session)` returning `{ session, user, profile, status }`

Replace `refreshProfile()` with `refreshUser()`. Profile-only refresh is forbidden because it can leave `session/user` and `profile/status` out of sync.

`refreshUser()` requirements:
1. Set auth state to a transient refreshing state.
2. Call `supabase.auth.getSession()`. If it errors or returns no session, demote to guest immediately.
3. Call `supabase.auth.getUser()` for authoritative identity. If it errors or returns no user, perform local sign-out cleanup and demote to guest.
4. Fetch the `profiles` row for that user. If the row is missing, query fails, or role parsing fails, perform local cleanup and demote to guest.
5. Derive terminal auth status from `profile.role` only.
6. Commit `session`, `user`, `profile`, and `status` atomically in one store update.
7. On any thrown exception, clear auth-scoped query data, stop active queue flush, and demote to `GUEST`.

Lifecycle map:
- App bootstrap: if no stored session, set guest; if session exists, call `refreshUser()`.
- `SIGNED_IN`, `TOKEN_REFRESHED`, `USER_UPDATED`: call `refreshUser()`.
- `SIGNED_OUT`: capture previous user id, set guest, clear return intent, clear that user’s offline queue bucket, clear auth-scoped cached queries.
- Provider must not redirect. [useProtectedRoute.ts](C:/Users/T/.codex/worktrees/e8ab/PeeDom/src/hooks/useProtectedRoute.ts) remains the only redirect/replay owner.

**3. Offline Mutation Queue Logic**

Add this type in the shared domain types:
- `type MutationOutcome = 'completed' | 'auth_required' | 'queued_retry'`

Refactor the queue architecture:
- Keep persistence in [offline-queue.ts](C:/Users/T/.codex/worktrees/e8ab/PeeDom/src/lib/offline-queue.ts), but stop using a single flat array filtered on initialize.
- Persist as `Record<userId, QueuedMutation[]>` under the existing storage key, or use one storage key per user. Do not keep the current filtered-array model; it can overwrite another user’s pending mutations on persist.
- `user_id` must be assigned by the queue layer from the active auth store, never passed from UI payloads.
- Every payload must be Zod-validated on enqueue and again on load before replay.

Introduce:
- `C:\Users\T\.codex\worktrees\e8ab\PeeDom\src\features\sync\mutationRegistry.ts`
- `C:\Users\T\.codex\worktrees\e8ab\PeeDom\src\hooks\useOfflineSync.ts`

`mutationRegistry` contract:
- map `MutationType` to `schema`, `executeRemote`, and `invalidateKeys`
- replay uses the same API functions as live mutations, not duplicate Supabase code

TanStack Query integration:
- `onMutate`: cancel relevant queries, snapshot previous cache, apply optimistic update, return rollback context.
- `mutationFn`: call a shared executor that returns `MutationOutcome` instead of throwing for queueable/auth cases.
- `onSuccess`: branch on `MutationOutcome`.
- `onError`: handle only permanent non-retryable failures.
- `onSettled`: invalidate queue-meta keys.

Shared executor rules:
1. Read `currentUserId` from `useAuthStore.getState()`.
2. If there is no authenticated user, return `auth_required`.
3. If device is offline before request, enqueue and return `queued_retry`.
4. If online, execute the remote mutation.
5. If the remote call fails due to auth/session loss, return `auth_required`.
6. If the remote call fails due to transient network/timeout/5xx behavior, enqueue and return `queued_retry`.
7. If the remote call succeeds, return `completed`.
8. If the remote call fails due to validation or business-rule rejection, throw and let `onError` rollback.

Outcome handling:
- `completed`: keep optimistic state and invalidate domain queries.
- `queued_retry`: keep optimistic state, mark pending-sync UI state, invalidate queue-count/pending-sync queries, show “Saved. Will sync when online.”
- `auth_required`: rollback optimistic state, set return intent if the action is replayable, route to login.
- `onError`: rollback optimistic state and show permanent failure. Do not enqueue in `onError`.

`user_id` flush rules:
- `flushForUser(userId)` may run only when `refreshUser()` has succeeded and the current authenticated store user id still equals `userId`.
- Never flush as guest.
- Never flush bucket `A` while authenticated as user `B`.
- If auth changes during a flush, stop after the current item, persist remaining items back into bucket `A`, and abort.
- On explicit sign-out, clear only the signed-out user’s bucket and auth-scoped optimistic cache.
- On `refreshUser()` failure, demote to guest and do not flush. Leave queued items dormant under their original `user_id`; they may only replay after that same user authenticates again.

Reference basis for Sentry config: [Expo Sentry guide](https://docs.expo.dev/guides/using-sentry/) and [Sentry Expo manual setup](https://docs.sentry.io/platforms/react-native/manual-setup/expo/).

Spec Complete. Ready for Engineering Execution.

Create this structure:
- `C:\Users\T\.codex\worktrees\e8ab\PeeDom\src\features\auth\useAuthStore.ts`
- `C:\Users\T\.codex\worktrees\e8ab\PeeDom\src\features\auth\AuthService.ts`
- `C:\Users\T\.codex\worktrees\e8ab\PeeDom\src\features\auth\AuthProvider.tsx`
- `C:\Users\T\.codex\worktrees\e8ab\PeeDom\src\features\auth\index.ts`

Transition rule:
- Reduce [AuthContext.tsx](C:/Users/T/.codex/worktrees/e8ab/PeeDom/src/contexts/AuthContext.tsx) to a compatibility export only during migration, then delete it once imports are moved.

Responsibilities:
- `useAuthStore`: readable auth state only. Hold `status`, `session`, `user`, `profile`, `loading`, `lastAuthError`, and derived selectors like `isGuest`, `isAuthenticated`, `canAccessProtectedRoute`.
- `AuthProvider`: owns bootstrap, `supabase.auth.onAuthStateChange`, queue/user-scope handoff, and cleanup. It does not fetch directly from components and does not navigate.
- `AuthService`: owns every Supabase call. No React imports. No Zustand imports.

`AuthService` must expose:
- `getSession()`
- `getUser()`
- `getProfile(userId)`
- `signInWithEmail(payload)`
- `signUpWithEmail(payload)`
- `signOutRemote()`
- `signOutLocal()`
- `resolveAuthSnapshot(session)` returning `{ session, user, profile, status }`

Replace `refreshProfile()` with `refreshUser()`. Profile-only refresh is forbidden because it can leave `session/user` and `profile/status` out of sync.

`refreshUser()` requirements:
1. Set auth state to a transient refreshing state.
2. Call `supabase.auth.getSession()`. If it errors or returns no session, demote to guest immediately.
3. Call `supabase.auth.getUser()` for authoritative identity. If it errors or returns no user, perform local sign-out cleanup and demote to guest.
4. Fetch the `profiles` row for that user. If the row is missing, query fails, or role parsing fails, perform local cleanup and demote to guest.
5. Derive terminal auth status from `profile.role` only.
6. Commit `session`, `user`, `profile`, and `status` atomically in one store update.
7. On any thrown exception, clear auth-scoped query data, stop active queue flush, and demote to `GUEST`.

Lifecycle map:
- App bootstrap: if no stored session, set guest; if session exists, call `refreshUser()`.
- `SIGNED_IN`, `TOKEN_REFRESHED`, `USER_UPDATED`: call `refreshUser()`.
- `SIGNED_OUT`: capture previous user id, set guest, clear return intent, clear that user’s offline queue bucket, clear auth-scoped cached queries.

**3. Offline Mutation Queue Logic**

Add this type in the shared domain types:
- `type MutationOutcome = 'completed' | 'auth_required' | 'queued_retry'`

Refactor the queue architecture:
- Keep persistence in [offline-queue.ts](C:/Users/T/.codex/worktrees/e8ab/PeeDom/src/lib/offline-queue.ts), but stop using a single flat array filtered on initialize.
- Persist as `Record<userId, QueuedMutation[]>` under the existing storage key, or use one storage key per user. Do not keep the current filtered-array model; it can overwrite another user’s pending mutations on persist.
- `user_id` must be assigned by the queue layer from the active auth store, never passed from UI payloads.
- Every payload must be Zod-validated on enqueue and again on load before replay.

Introduce:
- `C:\Users\T\.codex\worktrees\e8ab\PeeDom\src\features\sync\mutationRegistry.ts`
- `C:\Users\T\.codex\worktrees\e8ab\PeeDom\src\hooks\useOfflineSync.ts`

`mutationRegistry` contract:
- map `MutationType` to `schema`, `executeRemote`, and `invalidateKeys`
- replay uses the same API functions as live mutations, not duplicate Supabase code

TanStack Query integration:
- `onMutate`: cancel relevant queries, snapshot previous cache, apply optimistic update, return rollback context.
- `mutationFn`: call a shared executor that returns `MutationOutcome` instead of throwing for queueable/auth cases.
- `onSuccess`: branch on `MutationOutcome`.
- `onError`: handle only permanent non-retryable failures.
- `onSettled`: invalidate queue-meta keys.

Shared executor rules:
1. Read `currentUserId` from `useAuthStore.getState()`.
2. If there is no authenticated user, return `auth_required`.
3. If device is offline before request, enqueue and return `queued_retry`.
4. If online, execute the remote mutation.
5. If the remote call fails due to auth/session loss, return `auth_required`.
6. If the remote call fails due to transient network/timeout/5xx behavior, enqueue and return `queued_retry`.
7. If the remote call succeeds, return `completed`.
8. If the remote call fails due to validation or business-rule rejection, throw and let `onError` rollback.

Outcome handling:
- `completed`: keep optimistic state and invalidate domain queries.
- `queued_retry`: keep optimistic state, mark pending-sync UI state, invalidate queue-count/pending-sync queries, show “Saved. Will sync when online.”
- `auth_required`: rollback optimistic state, set return intent if the action is replayable, route to login.
- `onError`: rollback optimistic state and show permanent failure. Do not enqueue in `onError`.

`user_id` flush rules:
- `flushForUser(userId)` may run only when `refreshUser()` has succeeded and the current authenticated store user id still equals `userId`.
- Never flush as guest.
- Never flush bucket `A` while authenticated as user `B`.
- If auth changes during a flush, stop after the current item, persist remaining items back into bucket `A`, and abort.
- On explicit sign-out, clear only the signed-out user’s bucket and auth-scoped optimistic cache.
- On `refreshUser()` failure, demote to guest and do not flush. Leave queued items dormant under their original `user_id`; they may only replay after that same user authenticates again.

Reference basis for Sentry config: [Expo Sentry guide](https://docs.expo.dev/guides/using-sentry/) and [Sentry Expo manual setup](https://docs.sentry.io/platforms/react-native/manual-setup/expo/).
